claim: There are two fundamentally different kinds of agentic systems: ad hoc workflows (disposable process) and embedded workflows (process matters as much as output)
actionable: Classify your agent use case early (ad hoc vs embedded); embedded workflows need governance, audit trails, and human-in-the-loop approval.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | João Moura: Building Recurring, Governed, and Embedded Enterprise Workflows

claim: When adopting agents, think in terms of reusable building blocks (flywheels)—anything you build must be reusable by humans and agents
actionable: Design every tool, skill, and workflow for reuse across your organization; avoid one-off solutions.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | João Moura: Building Recurring, Governed, and Embedded Enterprise Workflows

claim: Building is becoming commoditized; the real value in agentic systems is in deployment, scaling, governance, and observability
actionable: Focus engineering resources on production-grade deployments, monitoring, and governance rather than building custom frameworks.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | João Moura: Building Recurring, Governed, and Embedded Enterprise Workflows

claim: Use email notifications for human-in-the-loop approval rather than forcing users to visit external apps—this drives adoption
actionable: Implement email-based human approval loops; users respond to email, agents pick up response; track SLAs and compliance.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | João Moura: Building Recurring, Governed, and Embedded Enterprise Workflows

claim: You must be able to zoom out (org-level metrics: cost, execution count, health) and zoom in (individual traces) in your observability system
actionable: Implement dual-level observability: org dashboards for health/cost, and trace inspection for debugging individual agent runs.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | João Moura: Building Recurring, Governed, and Embedded Enterprise Workflows

claim: Adopting agents successfully requires both a clear strategy and the ability to drive adoption across the organization
actionable: Define your agent strategy early, prioritize use cases by ROI, and invest in change management to drive user adoption.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | João Moura: Building Recurring, Governed, and Embedded Enterprise Workflows

claim: Use discovery-driven approaches to identify high-ROI use cases before committing engineering resources to agent development
actionable: Use CrewAI's discovery tools or similar to analyze your workflows and surface the most impactful agent opportunities.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | João Moura: Building Recurring, Governed, and Embedded Enterprise Workflows

claim: Self-improving agents (entangled agents) that build memory and learn from usage will be a major trend; design for this from the start
actionable: When designing agents, plan for self-improvement: long-running agents, memory building, autonomous skill creation, conversational first.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | João Moura: Building Recurring, Governed, and Embedded Enterprise Workflows
